pr.title: gr/gm: decompile stage and game functions
pr.author: johnwinston

summary: Decompiled seven stage/game functions across adventure-mode gmregclear, Hyrule Castle, and Onett ground code, with corresponding header prototype updates and small struct/field refinements. The PR body claims all functions reached 100% objdiff match and main.dol SHA1 OK; the bot report independently lists seven new 100% matches totaling +1072 matched-code bytes.

key_files.role: Replaced placeholders for fn_8017E318, gm_8017E7FC, gm_80180B18, and gm_80181A44 with matched C; refined nearby local structs and formatting.
evidence_refs: changed_files: gmregclear.c added 82 deleted 11 hunks 7
diff: fn_8017E318 sums lbl_804706D8 entries in a 12-iteration loop
diff: gm_8017E7FC implements adventure condition and scene transition state
diff: gm_80180B18 loops over 0x1B entries and stores scaled main-lib values
diff: gm_80181A44 uses switch over arg1 values 0x21-0x26 to set byte ranges from lbl_803D8D08

key_files.role: Updated recovered function signatures from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete prototypes.
evidence_refs: diff: fn_8017E318 changed to int fn_8017E318(void)
diff: gm_80180B18 changed to void gm_80180B18(void)
diff: gm_80181A44 changed to void gm_80181A44(int c_kind, int, bool)

key_files.role: Decompiled grCastle_801CE860 and fn_801D0924; added camera include required by recovered body.
evidence_refs: changed_files: grcastle.c added 39 deleted 2 hunks 3
diff: added #include "cm/camera.h"
diff: grCastle_801CE860 advances castle animation timer and calls Camera_80030E44
diff: fn_801D0924 calls grDisplay_801C5DB0 then conditionally loops 12 castle3 entries with lb_800117F4

key_files.role: Updated fn_801D0924 prototype from unknown to concrete parameters and void return.
evidence_refs: diff: fn_801D0924 changed to void fn_801D0924(HSD_GObj*, int)
changed_files: gronett.c added 20 deleted 1 hunks 1
diff: grOnett_801E3CE4 uses GET_GROUND, GET_JOBJ, Ground_801C2ED0, grAnime_801C7FF8, resets gv fields/callbacks, and registers mpJointSetCb1 callbacks

key_files.role: Updated grOnett_801E40E4 callback prototype from unknown to concrete signature.
evidence_refs: diff: grOnett_801E40E4 changed to void grOnett_801E40E4(Ground*, s32, CollData*, s32, mpLib_GroundEnum, f32)

what_changed: Seven placeholder functions were replaced with C implementations: grCastle_801CE860, fn_801D0924, grOnett_801E3CE4, fn_8017E318, gm_8017E7FC, gm_80180B18, and gm_80181A44.
Headers were made more truthful by replacing several UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete return types and parameters in gmregclear.h, grcastle.h, and gronett.h.
gmregclear.c received small struct layout refinements, including replacing padding at offset 0x108 with s16 x108 and adding byte fields around offsets 0x118-0x11B.
The bot report recorded seven new 100% matched items and +1072 bytes of matched code, plus a small .sdata2 improvement in grcastle.

smart_moves.text: The PR updated owning headers alongside recovered function bodies instead of leaving unknown prototypes in place.
evidence_refs: diff: gmregclear.h prototype updates for fn_8017E318, gm_80180B18, gm_80181A44
diff: grcastle.h prototype update for fn_801D0924
diff: gronett.h prototype update for grOnett_801E40E4

smart_moves.text: Repeated operations were expressed as bounded for-loops where the diff shows regular index ranges, such as 12 entries in fn_8017E318/fn_801D0924 and 0x1B entries in gm_80180B18.
evidence_refs: diff: for (i = 0; i < 12; i++) in fn_8017E318
diff: for (i = 0; i < 12; i++) in fn_801D0924
diff: for (i = 0; i < 0x1B; i++) in gm_80180B18

smart_moves.text: The gm_80181A44 range dispatch was represented as a switch over known selector values rather than a generated-looking branch ladder.
evidence_refs: diff: gm_80181A44 switch cases 0x21 through 0x26

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a recovered body proves a function signature, update the owning header in the same PR so callsites no longer depend on UNK_RET or UNK_PARAMS.
evidence_refs: diff: gmregclear.h replaced three unknown prototypes with concrete signatures
diff: grcastle.h replaced fn_801D0924 unknown prototype
diff: gronett.h replaced grOnett_801E40E4 unknown prototype

decomp_lessons.lesson: Small fixed-size table operations in gm/gr code can match cleanly as ordinary counted for-loops when the operation is regular over array entries.
evidence_refs: diff: fn_8017E318 loops 12 lbl_804706D8 entries
diff: gm_80180B18 loops 0x1B entries
diff: fn_801D0924 loops 12 castle3 entries
PR body: all functions at 100% match via objdiff
bot report: listed these functions as 100.00% after

decomp_lessons.lesson: Stage setup functions may require using existing access macros and callbacks, such as GET_GROUND/GET_JOBJ and mpJointSetCb1, when reconstructing source-level initialization.
evidence_refs: diff: grOnett_801E3CE4 uses GET_GROUND(gobj), GET_JOBJ(gobj), and mpJointSetCb1
bot report: grOnett_801E3CE4 reached 100.00%

naming_conventions.convention: Address-style global/function names were retained where semantic names were not established, while clear local role names such as gp, gobj, renderpass, timer, base, idx, sum, and rules were used inside recovered functions.
evidence_refs: diff: functions keep names fn_8017E318, gm_8017E7FC, gm_80180B18, gm_80181A44, grCastle_801CE860, fn_801D0924, grOnett_801E3CE4
diff: local variables include gp, timer, base, idx, sum, rules, renderpass

naming_conventions.convention: Unproven struct fields mostly remained offset-style names such as xC4, xC8, x14, x77, and lbl_80472E48 rather than receiving speculative semantic names.
evidence_refs: diff: gp->gv.castle.xC4/xC8
diff: lbl_80472E48.x14
diff: r31->x77

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was used narrowly in two matched stage functions.
evidence_refs: diff: PAD_STACK(4) in fn_801D0924
diff: PAD_STACK(8) in grOnett_801E3CE4
PR body: all functions at 100% match via objdiff
bot report: fn_801D0924 and grOnett_801E3CE4 listed at 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: The PR evidence supports that these uses were present in matched functions, but does not show why the padding was necessary or whether alternate cleanup was attempted.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used switch dispatch for gm_80181A44 selector cases 0x21-0x26 with a preserved base pointer to lbl_803D8D08.
evidence_refs: diff: u8* base assigned lbl_803D8D08 before switch
diff: switch cases assign (base + offset)[c_kind] = arg2
bot report: gm_80181A44 listed at 100.00%
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Consistent with canonical control-flow cleanup, but the slice does not include review discussion proving this was compared against other forms.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Recovered field accesses and includes to support typed source, such as Ground* gp from gobj->user_data/GET_GROUND and adding cm/camera.h for Camera_80030E44.
evidence_refs: diff: Ground* gp = gobj->user_data in grCastle_801CE860 and fn_801D0924
diff: Ground* gp = GET_GROUND(gobj) in grOnett_801E3CE4
diff: added #include "cm/camera.h"
assembly_or_matching_tactics.notes: Some raw pointer arithmetic remains in grCastle_801CE860 and gm_80181A44; the PR evidence does not prove whether stronger typed data structures were available.

notes: No review comments were present in the loaded slice, so review_feedback is empty.
The PR body claims all functions matched and main.dol SHA1 OK; the bot report corroborates seven 100% function matches, but the slice does not include raw objdiff logs.
PAD_STACK use is visible in matched functions, but necessity and attempted alternatives are not documented in comments or review.
Some field names and raw pointer arithmetic are visible in the diff, but the slice does not establish whether better type recovery was possible.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recovered bodies should drive header prototype cleanup
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2292 decompiled several gm/gr functions and updated the owning headers from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to concrete signatures in the same change, with bot-reported 100% matches for the affected functions.
evidence_refs: diff: gmregclear.h, grcastle.h, and gronett.h prototype changes
text_corpus: bot report lists seven new 100.00% matches

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use natural counted loops for regular fixed tables
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2292 matched fixed-size table operations using simple counted for-loops in fn_8017E318, gm_80180B18, and fn_801D0924.
evidence_refs: diff: for-loop over 12 lbl_804706D8 entries
diff: for-loop over 0x1B gm_80164024 entries
diff: for-loop over 12 castle3 entries

source_update_candidates.title: Document that matched PAD_STACK uses still need rationale when retained
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2292 contains narrow PAD_STACK uses in two functions that are reported as 100% matched, but the slice does not explain why the padding is necessary. Consider reinforcing that PAD_STACK should be accompanied by a note or verifier evidence when it remains in source.
evidence_refs: diff: PAD_STACK(4) in fn_801D0924
diff: PAD_STACK(8) in grOnett_801E3CE4
PR body: all functions at 100% match via objdiff
source_update_candidates.reason: This supports an existing standards theme rather than proving a new accepted global rule.

source_update_candidates.title: gmregclear/grcastle/gronett are active decomp-matching areas for adventure and stage systems
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2292 changed src/melee/gm/gmregclear.c/.h, src/melee/gr/grcastle.c/.h, and src/melee/gr/gronett.c/.h to decompile seven game/stage functions.
evidence_refs: changed_files.jsonl: six changed files
human_pr_text: summary lists adventure mode, Hyrule Castle, and Onett functions
source_update_candidates.reason: Useful for source path classification, but should be reviewed before adding as path-fact metadata.

rejection_notes: Do not infer reviewer approval details beyond the PR's merged state; no reviews or review comments were loaded.
Do not generalize that PAD_STACK is preferred style; the evidence only shows it was present in two matched functions.
Do not rename lbl_/fn_/offset-style symbols based on this PR alone; the diff largely preserves conservative names.
Do not claim raw pointer arithmetic in grCastle_801CE860 or gm_80181A44 is ideal typed style; the slice only proves it was part of the matched submitted source.
